ActionOrigin.description:
Origin of an API action — tracks who initiated the operation.

Used by the orchestrator notification middleware to provide context
about what triggered a side-effect API call.

oneOf.description: A human interacting through a UI surface

cwd.description:
Working directory of the UI request, used for project-scope
attribution of the action (#89). The WebUI/TUI passes the cwd from
the request body so a server-side `ActionPerformed` carries its
originating project even when the caller is not tracked in
`state.agents`.

oneOf.description: An AI agent (orchestrator or worker) via MCP tools

cwd.description:
Caller working directory at origin creation time (MCP loopback only).

Set by `TmaiHttpClient::from_runtime` via `std::env::current_dir()`
so a server-side `ActionPerformed` carries its originating project
even when the MCP agent ID is not tracked in `state.agents` (#75).

is_producer.description:
Whether this agent is the Producer.

Wire field name `is_producer` (DR
`2026-05-16-producer-identity-and-operator-addressing` §B —
the deliberate contract-surface change that #402's neutral
`is_orchestrator` rename was holding back). `default` +
`skip_serializing_if` make it optional: an old client that
omits the field still deserializes (→ `false`) and the field
is absent on the wire when `false`, so neither side is forced
into lockstep.

oneOf.description: An automated system process (auto_cleanup, pr_monitor, etc.)

cwd.description:
Working directory of the originating subsystem process, used for
project-scope routing (#89).

AgentAttention.description:
What the agent is waiting for. Three variants; absence (`Option::None`)
means "running normally — no UI signal needed".

Hint-only for UI / audit consumers; Core / PTY-server / Hub must never
branch on the discriminant (decision §3 wire contract from the
attention rebuild, preserved by the 2026-05-09 enum simplification).

AimCreateRequest.description:
`POST /api/units/{unit}/aims` request — create a new aim node (graduation
Stage 2-A). `state` defaults to `open` server-side (not on the wire), the
body starts empty, and no cross-edges are written. `slug` is the
operator-chosen, NON-dated, kebab / filename-safe, **unique** node identity
(`doc/aims/<slug>.md`).

parent.description: Parent node slug; `null` for a root node. If set, must be an existing slug.

slug.description: Filename stem — the stable, NON-dated node identity.

AimDriftWire.description:
Stale-from-ancestor drift coordinates for one aim node — the wire projection
of [`crate::workbench::compose::AimDriftItem`] (parent-only v1). Dates are
ISO-8601 strings (the convention [`super::approaches_view::ApproachWire`]
set). The ancestor's commit *subject* is intentionally dropped: the panel
needs the which-ancestor / when coordinates, not the prose.

aim_change_date.description: ISO-8601 date this node itself last changed.

ancestor_change_date.description: ISO-8601 date the ancestor's anchor moved.

ancestor_change_sha.description: Short sha of the commit that moved the ancestor's `aim:` anchor.

stale_from_ancestor_slug.description:
The ancestor (on the `parent` chain) whose `aim:` anchor moved most
recently after this node last changed.

AimEditRequest.description:
`PUT /api/units/{unit}/aims/{slug}` request — the operator's frontmatter edit
(aim-tree graduation Stage 2-A). Carries **only** the three editable fields;
the cross-edges (`depends_on` / `serves` / `related`) and the body are
preserved server-side (cross-edge editing is a deferred increment). Authority
is operator-only, *soft* — like the in-tmai merge button, there is no
draft/accept gate beyond the normal endpoint auth.

parent.description: Parent node slug (tree skeleton); `null` re-roots the node.

AimInteriorKind.description:
The kind of an interior mark — mirrors the `doc/aims/README.md` convention:
`confirmed` = external ground-truth checked (test / PR / git ref), real,
attention-zero; `claimed` = unverified, an operator confirm is owed;
`pruned` = a rejected branch kept in the tree with its rejection reason
(the recoil-ledger convention, `doc/aims/aim-recoil-ledger.md`). The
projection records which kind the author wrote — it never re-judges it.
In particular, `pruned` is valid on any node: pairing it with
`state: dead` is authoring convention, not a parser rule.

AimInteriorWire.description:
One interior mark projected to the wire — a single `[claimed]` /
`[confirmed: <ref>]` / `[pruned: <reason>]` line from the node body. `text`
is the line's prose with the marker (and any leading list bullet) stripped;
`reference` carries the colon payload — the confirmed ref (the test / PR /
git ref) or the pruned rejection reason — and is `null` for a bare
`[claimed]` / `[pruned]` (or a `[claimed — note]`, whose note is
commentary, not a ref). Wire key for `reference` is `ref` (mirroring
[`super::approaches_view::ReviewTriggerWire`]).

AimState.description:
Lifecycle state of an aim node.

Per `doc/aims/README.md`: `done` = the aim was reached / confirmed; `dead` =
self-death (the means failed; the lineage stays, the parent is untouched).
Unlike a decision's `status`, this is a node-local fact, not an authority
gate — the machine marks, it never appraises.

AimWire.description:
One aim node projected to the wire — the human-side complement of the
internal [`crate::workbench::aim::AimRecord`]. Carries the full node: the
human bearing (`aim`), the tree skeleton (`parent`), the DAG cross-edges
(`depends_on` / `serves` / `related`, slugs), the lifecycle `state`, and the
agent-authored interior (`body`).

Field names are snake_case on the wire (mirroring the sibling views'
`repo_root` etc.); the cross-edge keys (`depends_on`) match the corpus
frontmatter spelling, so the wire and the records stay one-to-one.

body.description:
The markdown interior (everything after the frontmatter), for the
R-panel detail pane. May be empty (a frontmatter-only node). Kept verbatim
— [`Self::interior`] is an ADDITIVE projection over it, not a replacement.

depends_on.description: DAG cross-edges (slugs): "depends on". Empty when absent.

oneOf.description:
Stale-from-ancestor drift, if this node drifted (some ancestor's `aim:`
anchor moved after the node last changed). `null` / absent when current.
Computed on read by reusing [`crate::workbench::compose::aim_drift`] — the
same parent-only (v1) verdict the Producer hand-over's `tmai-aim-drift`
fence carries (one source of truth). Populated **only** by the read
endpoint; [`Self::from_record`] leaves it `None`, because the single-record
write endpoints have no corpus context to walk the ancestor chain.

is.description:
Interior `is[]` — the marked claims parsed from [`Self::body`]: each
`[claimed]` / `[confirmed: <ref>]` / `[pruned: <reason>]` line in the
interior prose projected to one entry. **Mark-only** (design pin #1 /
`doc/aims/README.md`): the projection surfaces what the author wrote
(confirmed / claimed / pruned) and never re-judges, re-orders, or
de-drifts it. Empty when the interior carries no markers. Wire key is
`is` (the corpus spelling).

parent.description: Parent node slug (tree skeleton); `null` for a root node.

related.description: DAG cross-edges (slugs): loose relations. Empty when absent.

slug.description: Filename stem — the stable, NON-dated node identity (e.g. `aim-system`).

oneOf.description:
Working-tree presence facts for this node (Design B of
`doc/aims/aim-drift-commit-boundary.md`): uncommitted edits / an
uncommitted `aim:`-anchor edit / an untracked new node. A SEPARATE
field and type from [`Self::drift`] on purpose — the committed layer
states order judgments, this layer states presence only. `null` /
absent when the working tree is clean for this node. Populated **only**
by the read endpoint; [`Self::from_record`] leaves it `None`.

AimWorkingDeltaWire.description:
Working-tree presence facts for one aim node — the wire projection of
[`crate::workbench::compose::AimWorkingDeltaItem`] (Design B of
`doc/aims/aim-drift-commit-boundary.md`). Deliberately a separate type from
[`AimDriftWire`]: the committed layer states ORDER judgments (it carries
commit coordinates), this layer states PRESENCE only — there is no date /
sha / ordering field here because a working tree has none to state.

uncommitted.description: The node's file differs from HEAD (uncommitted edits, staged or not).

uncommitted_anchor_change.description:
The uncommitted diff touches the node's `aim:` line. Implies
`uncommitted`.

untracked.description:
A new node with no committed history. Mutually exclusive with
`uncommitted`.

AimsResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/aims` response — the unit's aim-tree surface, per
repo, JSON-projected. Composed on demand (no cache). Carries every aim
record regardless of `state`; the R panel builds / filters the tree
client-side, mirroring the approaches / decisions / observations dashboards.

For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1`.

ApproachInventoryWire.description:
One approach's projected inventory row, mirroring [`ApproachEntry`]. An
approach serving N decisions is cloned into each serving decision's nest
(and into the unanchored list if it serves none in scope) — the same shape
either way.

display.description: Slug with the `YYYY-MM-DD-` prefix stripped, for compact display.

projected_status.description: Status projected from the approach's fact-log.

ApproachStatus.description:
Lifecycle position of an approach record.

Unlike [`super::decision::DecisionStatus`], the approach lifecycle resolves
outward (`planned → partial → ready → running → validated / rejected /
replaced`) rather than through a supersede chain. The old `active` state
was found (#462 Amendment 2026-05-28) to smuggle two distinct layers into
one enum value — the intrinsic phase of the approach (`(α)` "is the
success-signal armed?") and the operator's stance (`(β)` "am I currently
watching this?"). Splitting it into `Ready` and `Running` keeps the enum
honest to layer `(α)` only: documents speak about themselves, not about
whether they are being observed.

A validated / rejected approach is preserved as the audit-trail record of
*what we tried and what happened*; a follow-up decision (if any) is
authored separately rather than in-place.

See `doc/approaches/2026-05-27-approach-lifecycle-planned-and-partial-states.md`,
Amendment 2026-05-28 for the `Ready` / `Running` split and the dashboard
全件可視化 direction.

ApproachWire.description:
One approach projected to the wire — the human-side complement of the
internal [`crate::workbench::compose::ApproachView`]. The `status`
field is the lifecycle position (`Planned` / `Partial` / `Ready` /
`Running` / `Validated` / `Rejected` / `Replaced`) the dashboard
reads to bucket / sort.

oneOf.description: Producer confidence; `None` when no confidence is on record.

replaced_by.description:
Approach slugs that replaced this one — meaningful only when
`status: replaced`.

review_history.description: Extend ledger — empty for fresh approaches.

review_triggers.description:
Re-evaluation triggers, non-empty per parse-time validation. The
single-date `review_after:` sugar is already expanded into a `Date`
trigger by the parser.

ApproachesResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/approaches` response — the unit's approaches
surface, per repo, JSON-projected. Composed on demand (no cache).
Carries every approach record regardless of status; the dashboard
filters / sorts client-side per #462 Amendment 2026-05-28.

For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1` — the
common case until per-unit `also[]` per-repo `approaches_dir`
overrides land (tracked in tmai-core#340 for the multi-repo follow-up
alongside the decisions-side override).

BundleId.description:
Stable identifier for a dispatch bundle (a logical grouping of
sub-dispatches — e.g. "apply the same change across 4 repos").

Serialized transparently as a plain string so that existing log and
event consumers can treat the field as an opaque identifier without
needing to know the wrapper type.

BundleStatus.description:
Rollup status of a dispatch bundle, emitted by
[`CoreEvent::BundleStatusChanged`].

Derived from its sub-dispatches; transitions here are coarser-grained
than the per-agent `AgentStatusChanged` stream (issue #463, open
question 3).

CalibrationCellWire.description:
One row of the calibration table, flattened from the workbench's
`BTreeMap<(verdict, confidence), Cell>` for JSON serialization.

JSON cannot key on a tuple; the rendered shape is a list of objects so
the React client iterates without having to reconstruct the map shape.

CalibrationEntry.description:
One entry in the calibration store: a single triage decision plus its
later-observed objective outcome (or none yet).

note_source.description:
Source the verdict came from, e.g. a worker friction note filename
(`20260513-1500-baz.md`) or a drift-audit item slug. Relative to the
unit's feedback directory; opaque to this module.

oneOf.description:
Objective outcome, if one has been picked up since (stage 5).
Absent in fresh entries; serialized only when present.

rationale.description: One-line rationale from the Producer, for human review later.

synthesis_pass_id.description:
UUID of the synthesis pass that produced this verdict. String, not
`uuid::Uuid`, so older entries with non-UUID ids stay parseable.

tier_routed.description:
Which authority the friction was routed to, encoded as a `u8` so the
dormant on-disk store and the ts-rs/openapi wire stay byte-stable:
`1` = the friction implies a *decision-act* → human-gated tripwire
(the Producer flags, never decides); `2` = *approach-execution* → the
Producer may handle. The `DecisionFrontmatter::tier` field this once
mirrored has been removed — authority now derives from slot + category
+ act, per `doc/decisions/2026-05-16-authority-attaches-to-the-act.md`.

CalibrationResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/calibration` response — read-only projection of
the unit's calibration store.

`bootstrap_threshold` mirrors `workbench::BOOTSTRAP_ENTRY_THRESHOLD` so
the client can show the "lean toward asking the human" caveat without
re-encoding the constant. `tier1_violations` mirrors what the hand-over
digest's `⚡ TIER-1 TRIPWIRE TRIGGERED` callout surfaces (DR §B.4 zero
tolerance) — so polling this endpoint is enough to drive a top-bar
tripwire indicator in the WebUI without depending on hand-over compose
timing.

CapacityCauseSummary.description:
What caused a [`CoreEvent::CapacityChanged`] emission.

Subscribers use this to attribute the capacity delta without needing
a separate query. `delta == 0` is valid (e.g. `LimitChanged` when a
config reload moved the ceiling but no slots moved).

oneOf.description: An agent was spawned; consumed a slot.

target.description: Target of the newly spawned agent.

oneOf.description: An agent reached a terminal state (exit/kill); freed a slot.

target.description: Target of the agent that ended.

oneOf.description: The configured capacity limit changed (reload / admin action).
Capacity snapshot was reconciled against live state (drift fix).

current.description: Number of agent slots currently in use.

Confidence.description:
The Producer's own confidence in a triage verdict. Binary by DR §B.1 —
when in doubt, [`Confidence::Low`].

`Ord` is derived (lexicographic on the variant order: `High` < `Low`) so
it can be a `BTreeMap` key for stable table rendering — order has no
numeric meaning. Same reasoning for [`TriageVerdict`] below.

CountsWire.description:
Bucket counts for one repo's `Settled` section — mirrors
`compose::Counts`.

CurrencyItemWire.description: One row of the `⚠ Currency sweep` callout.

DecisionCategory.description:
What kind of decision this is — drives the workbench's temperature treatment.

`category` is the *kind* (blast radius / abstraction), not the authority.
Authority is not a stored field: it is derived from the slot
(`doc/decisions/` ⇒ a human-accepted decision vs `doc/approaches/` ⇒ a
Producer-run experiment), `category: foundational`, and the act itself
(accepting a decision is a human gate; running an approach is the
Producer's). See `doc/decisions/2026-05-16-authority-attaches-to-the-act.md`.

DecisionInventoryWire.description:
One decision's projected inventory row with its serving approaches nested,
mirroring [`DecisionEntry`].

`frontmatter_status` is the decision's *frontmatter* lifecycle status
(`draft` / `proposed` / `accepted` / …) — orienting context, NOT a fact
projection. The #484 engine projects status from facts for *approaches*
only; a decision's only status is the asserted frontmatter one, so this
wire field carries it under its honest name rather than mislabelling it a
projection.

display.description: Slug with the `YYYY-MM-DD-` prefix stripped, for compact display.

frontmatter_status.description: The decision's frontmatter lifecycle status (display context).

running_count.description:
How many serving approaches project to `running` (feeds the `overflow`
render; a mechanical count, not an appraisal).

serving.description: Approaches whose `serves:` names this decision, projected + nested.

serving_health.description: Running-means verdict over the serving set.

DecisionWire.description:
One decision projected to the wire — the human-side complement of the
internal `compose::DecisionView`. `last_verified` is rendered as the
ISO-8601 date the frontmatter carries (e.g. `"2026-05-14"`).

excerpt.description:
For 🔴 in-play: the `## Decision` section of the body. For
everything else: a one-paragraph summary.

oneOf.description:
`Some` ONLY when this decision is "accepted (projection: pending)" — a
relay-gated accept-commit for its slug exists in the core-only δ branch
while `status` here (read from main) still says `proposed`. **Derived,
never stored** (relay-channel B3.3-a: no hidden second state — the δ
commit's existence IS the accept). Absent (skipped from JSON) otherwise,
so the wire stays lockstep-free and pre-P3 clients are unaffected.

P2 ships the field (wire readiness) but never populates it: the
derivation is **P3's** (B3 variant / projection) and is *not* an accept
gate. P2 keeps it `None`.

`Some` when a `governs:` path changed in git after `last_verified` —
the workbench's drift signal.

DecisionsResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/decisions` response — the `Settled` section of
the unit's hand-over, per repo, JSON-projected. Composed on demand
(no cache), so the client polls when it wants fresh data.

For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1`. For a
multi-repo unit (per
`doc/decisions/2026-05-14-producer-capability-valve-principle.md`
§C/§D) each repo is composed independently; the React side renders a
group per repo without any cross-repo aggregation.

composed_at.description: RFC3339 compose timestamp (the moment the server gathered + composed).

DetectionSource.description:
Source of agent state detection

Wire format is `snake_case` to match the contract-layer enums
(`VendorAvailabilityState`, `CapacityCauseSummary`, `BundleStatus`). The
PascalCase names (e.g. `"HttpHook"`) shipped prior to #7 are still
accepted on deserialization via `#[serde(alias = ...)]` so that
previously-persisted `MonitoredAgent.detection_source` payloads continue
to round-trip.

DiffSummarySnapshot.description: Diff statistics snapshot for API consumers

DispatchId.description:
Globally unique identifier for a dispatched unit of work.

Backed by UUID v7 so that records sort lexicographically by creation
time without needing a separate timestamp index. Serialized as a plain
string (`#[serde(transparent)]`) so external consumers can treat the
field as an opaque token without learning the wrapper type.

DispatchIntentSummary.description:
Redacted summary of a dispatch intent attached to
[`CoreEvent::DispatchRejected`] and other contract-layer events.

The full prompt and any credentials **must never** appear here — callers
that need the raw intent go through an out-of-band audit channel
(issue #463, open question 2). Consumers seeing `prompt_hash` can
correlate back to the original dispatch request without learning its
contents.

oneOf.description: Bundle id when this dispatch is part of a multi-project bundle.

issue_number.description: Associated GitHub issue number, if any.

pr_number.description: Associated pull request number, if any.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the target project / worktree root.

prompt_hash.description:
Hex digest (implementation-chosen hash, e.g. SHA-256) of the
dispatched prompt. Lets auditors correlate this event with the
recorded intent without exposing the prompt text.

DispatchKind.description:
Classification of what was dispatched. Drives notification phrasing,
gatekeeper rules (#11), and selector disambiguation (e.g. an
implementer worktree and a review worktree both reference the same PR
number — `kind` separates them).

oneOf.description: Work attached to a GitHub issue (no PR yet).
Work attached to an existing pull request (e.g. follow-up commits).
Free-form work not tied to an issue or PR.
A reviewer dispatched against an existing PR.
An orchestrator dispatch.

DispatchRefs.description:
Stable references attached to a dispatch at request time. These are
the fields a notification, audit log, or external observer should read
— not the live agent snapshot, which may be stale.

base_branch.description: Branch the worktree was forked from.

project_root.description:
Absolute path to the project the dispatch targets. Resolver scoping
uses this to prevent the cross-project id collisions described in
the issue.

title.description:
Human-readable title chosen at dispatch time so notifications
always have something to render even before the agent prints
anything.

worktree_name.description: Worktree name when the dispatch creates or attaches to a worktree.

DispatchSnapshot.description: UI-facing snapshot of a dispatched unit of work.

agent_target.description: Live agent tmux target (e.g. "main:0.1"), if the agent has been attached.

oneOf.description: Bundle id when this dispatch is part of a multi-repo bundle.

display_subtitle.description: Short subtitle derived from dispatch kind (e.g. "Issue #42").

duration_ms.description: Elapsed milliseconds from spawn to terminal state, if finished.

finished_at.description: Wall-clock time when the dispatch reached a terminal state, if any.

is_terminal.description: Whether the dispatch has reached a terminal state (Stopped / Failed).

kind.description: What kind of work was dispatched.

pty_session_id.description: PTY session id, if the agent was spawned via the PTY layer.

refs.description: Stable references captured at request time.

role.description: Agent role this dispatch was filled with.

spawner.description: Who or what originated this dispatch.

title.description: Human-readable title chosen at dispatch time.

DispatchState.description:
Lifecycle position of a dispatch.

Persisted alongside the dispatch record so a tmai restart can restore
the ledger without consulting any live process state.

oneOf.description: Created, not yet handed to a spawner.
Spawner is running but no agent process has been observed yet.
Live agent attached and producing output.
Live agent attached and idle (no in-flight tool call).
Paused waiting for a human or external signal.
Terminated normally.
Terminated abnormally.

EntityChange.description:
Whether an entity was upserted (created or updated) or removed.

Used in Entity-Update [`CoreEvent`] variants and [`crate::api::envelope::EntityUpdateEnvelope`].

ErrorCode.description:
Machine-readable error code — stable across releases.

Added codes **never** get removed: deprecations are handled with serde
aliases so old payloads still deserialize. Callers switch on this value
instead of `message` text.

content.description: File content (UTF-8 text; binary content is rejected upstream).

editable.description: `true` when the extension is one the markdown panel can open/edit.

path.description: The file path (echoed from the request).

repo.description: The repo the file was resolved within (echoed from the request).

FileTreeEntryWire.description:
One node in a unit repo's file tree. Mirrors the binary crate's
`MdTreeEntry` (the `/api/files/md-tree` walk output) as a wire type that
lives in `tmai-core` so it can be ts-exported; the handler maps the walk's
entries into this shape rather than duplicating the walk.

`children` is `Some` for directories (recursively), `None` for files.
`openable` marks the text extensions the markdown panel can open/edit.

children.description:
`no_recursion` breaks utoipa's schema walk on this self-reference —
without it `ApiDoc::openapi()` recurses into the type forever and
overflows the stack (utoipa 5 emits a `$ref` instead).

FoundationalDueWire.description:
One row of the `⚠ Trajectory check due` callout — a foundational
decision whose `last_verified` is older than
`workbench::FOUNDATIONAL_DUE_DAYS`.

HandoffEntryWire.description:
One hand-off entry in the list: the active baton or an archived one.

`status` distinguishes the active baton (`"active"`) from an archived one
(`"archived"`) — i.e. *where the baton lives*, deliberately NOT the
consumption lifecycle (`HandoffStatus` Unused/Consumed), which answers a
different question. `composed_at` / `task` are parsed from the baton's
optional YAML frontmatter; either is `None` when the frontmatter is absent
or omits that field (an unparseable archived baton is still listed, bare).

name.description:
`"active"` for the active baton, or the archive filename (e.g.
`2026-05-12T18-30-00.000Z.md`) for an archived one. This is the
`{name}` the content endpoint keys on.

status.description:
`"active"` or `"archived"` — where the baton lives, not its
consumption lifecycle.

allOf.description: Phase reached by this event (carries `reason` on `Escalate`).

message.description: Free-form human-readable context (omitted when absent on the wire).

new_agent_id.description: Canonical AgentId of the newly spawned Producer. Set only on `Ready`.

ritual_id.description: Fresh UUID assigned when the ritual started.

unit.description: Unit name the ritual is being run for.

HandoffRitualEvent.description:
SSE payload emitted at each transition of the
`POST /api/units/{unit}/handoff-and-restart` ritual.

Wired into [`CoreEvent::HandoffRitual`]; surfaces on `/api/events` as event
kind `handoff_ritual`. `ritual_id` is a fresh UUID minted at ritual start
so concurrent rituals across units (or operator retries) don't alias.

HandoffRitualPhase.description:
Phase of the Producer handoff-and-restart ritual (DR
`2026-05-14-handoff-lifecycle-and-kill-ux.md` §C; operator review gate
`2026-06-19` aim `handoff-review-gate`, #547).

Phases progress strictly in the order `Prompted → Validated →
AwaitingReview → Killed → Launching → Ready`; any step can short-circuit to
`Escalate` with a machine-readable `reason` (§D — uniform tier-1
escalation, no per-mode policy). A `request-rewrite` decision at the
`AwaitingReview` gate loops back to a fresh `Prompted → Validated →
AwaitingReview` round (the old Producer is re-prompted, never killed until
the operator approves). Consumers driving the WebUI overlay (`§E`) treat all
`Escalate` variants as the terminal failure state regardless of reason.

oneOf.description: Ritual prompt was delivered to the active Producer (§C step 2).
`active.md` baton landed fresh + validated (§C steps 3-4).

Baton validated; the ritual is PAUSED at the operator review gate
before the irreversible kill (#547 / aim `handoff-review-gate`). The
operator either approves (→ `Killed`) or requests a rewrite (→ the old
Producer is re-prompted and a fresh `Prompted → Validated →
AwaitingReview` round runs; the kill is never reached until approval).
The baton body is fetched out-of-band by the overlay
(`GET /api/units/{unit}/handoffs/active`), so this phase carries no
payload beyond the shared event envelope.
Old Producer was killed via PTY-server `kill_agent` (§C step 5).
New Producer spawn submitted (§C step 6); not yet `started`.
New Producer reported `attention = started`; ritual complete (§C step 7).

Failure path; `reason` is a machine-readable code per DR §D failure
inventory (`no_active_producer`, `multiple_producers`,
`prompt_send_failed`, `no_baton`, `malformed_handoff`, `kill_failed`,
`spawn_failed`, `review_abandoned`). Consumers route every variant to
the same operator dialog (§E).

HandoffsResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/handoffs` response — the unit's baton list.

The active baton (if any) sorts first; archived entries follow newest-first
(the order [`HandoffStore::archived`] yields). Empty `handoffs` when the
unit has neither an active baton nor any archive.

IssueLabelWire.description:
One issue label projected to the wire — a TS/utoipa-deriving mirror of
[`crate::github::IssueLabel`].

WHY this exists rather than re-using the domain `IssueLabel`: the domain
type derives only `serde`, NOT `ts_rs::TS` / `utoipa::ToSchema`. Giving the
`github::` domain types those derives would cascade new generated TS / spec
schemas whose shape must then be kept in lock-step with `gh`'s JSON — the
same downstream `tsc -b` break risk the `pr_view.rs` header comment
describes for the enum-ish PR fields. A thin wire mirror keeps the value
byte-identical to what the domain type serializes while leaving the domain
derive stack untouched.

IssueSummaryWire.description:
One issue projected to the wire. Field-for-field parity with
[`crate::github::IssueInfo`] so the React client renders a cross-repo entry
with the exact same component it would use for a per-repo list.

closed_at.description:
Close time (ISO-8601) — `null` while open. State-transition vocab
event (#524).

created_at.description:
Issue creation time (ISO-8601, `gh`'s `createdAt`) — the "new issue"
vocab event of the remote-Δ freshness design (#524). `Option` +
`serde(default)` with the `AimWire::working_delta` discipline so
older payloads (hub fixtures, pre-sync clients) stay deserializable.

LivenessWire.description:
Liveness of an approach's latest fact, mirroring [`Liveness`]. `last_fact`
is an ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` date (or `null` when the record has no facts); both
`last_fact` and `days_since` are `null` together in that no-facts case.

days_since.description: `today − last_fact` in whole days, or `null` when there are no facts.

last_fact.description: ISO date of the most recent fact, or `null` when the record has none.

stalled.description:
`true` once the latest fact is more than the engine's staleness window
old. A record with no facts is never stalled.

Outcome.description:
An *objective* outcome observed after a triage verdict, used as ground
truth for calibration. Per DR §B.2: the human's accept/reject is **not**
here — only failure signals the world produces independently.

The data is structurally complete in stage 1; the *detection* (scanning
git log / PR history to populate these) lands in stage 5.

oneOf.description:
A `Revert "<title>"` commit later touched a path this verdict bore on
— the absorbed change was wrong. Strongest false-negative signal.

A `fix:` commit shortly after touched a path this verdict bore on —
the absorbed change introduced a defect. Weaker signal (fixes happen
for many reasons), but still informative in aggregate.

CI failed on a PR shortly after, and a follow-up PR fixed it — the
absorbed change broke the build.

PendingProjectionWire.description:
Evidence that an accepted decision's projection is still pending: the
core-only δ branch carrying the attributable accept-commit, and that
commit's SHA. The pair is what P3 derives "accepted (projection: pending)"
from (a δ accept-commit exists while main still reads `proposed`); it carries
no stored state of its own.

accept_branch.description: The δ branch on which the accept-commit lives (e.g. `tmai-accept`).

accept_commit.description: Full SHA of the δ accept-commit whose existence IS the durable accept.

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode reported by Claude Code in hook payloads.

Maps to the session's current permission level (e.g., "default", "plan",
"dontAsk", "acceptEdits").

PrDiffResponse.description:
`GET /api/github/pr/diff` response — a PR's raw unified patch
(`gh pr diff <n> --patch`). Deliberately verbatim text, not a parsed
hunk AST: the diff *is* the contract; rendering structure is the React
viewer's concern (DR §A — review the code diff in-tmai).

patch.description:
Verbatim unified-diff text. Empty string is a valid response (a PR
whose head equals its base produces an empty patch).

repo.description: Absolute repo path the diff was read from (echoes the request).

PrSummaryWire.description:
One PR projected to the wire. Field-for-field parity with
[`crate::github::PrInfo`] (the single-repo `GET /api/github/prs` shape)
so the React client renders a cross-repo entry with the exact same
component it already uses for the per-repo list.

`review_decision` / `check_status` are carried as the raw uppercased
strings `gh` emits (`"APPROVED"`, `"CHANGES_REQUESTED"`, `"SUCCESS"`,
…) rather than typed enums. WHY: the domain enums
(`github::ReviewDecision` / `CheckStatus`) do not derive `ts_rs::TS` /
`utoipa::ToSchema`, and giving them those derives would cascade new
generated TS enums whose casing must then be kept in lock-step with
`gh`'s `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` serde repr — a downstream `tsc -b` break
risk for zero UI gain (the viewer only displays the badge text). A
string passthrough keeps the value byte-identical to what
`GET /api/github/prs` already serializes; the conversion below derives
it from the very same `serde` impl, so the two endpoints can never drift.

check_status.description:
Raw rolled-up check status (`"SUCCESS"` / `"FAILURE"` / `"PENDING"`),
or `null` when there are no checks.

ci_completed_at.description:
Max `completedAt` across the status-check rollup once it is terminal
(SUCCESS / FAILURE); `null` while pending or without checks. The "CI
conclusion" vocab event (#524). Deliberately NOT a raw `updated_at`:
comment/label churn must not look like a vocab event.

ci_local_attestation.description:
The ci-local attestation the Producer stored alongside the delivered
marker, if any (approach `2026-05-26-producer-supplied-override-
attestation.md`, O2-d). `Some` only when `producer_reviewed` is true
AND the Producer supplied an attestation via `mark_pr_reviewed`.

Neutral evidence-of-verification, **not** an approval — same §E
boundary as `producer_reviewed`. The react half (Phase B) reads it to
pre-fill the billing-dead override textarea so the operator need not
hand-paste the ci-local summary; absence simply means the operator
falls back to the manual paste (the O2-a path). The backend consumes
the *stored* copy directly — this wire field is for the UI, not the
authoritative override input.

Optional / serde-default with the same lockstep-free discipline as
`producer_reviewed`: absent on the wire when `None`, and a payload
omitting it deserializes to `None`.

closed_at.description:
Close time (ISO-8601) — `null` while open; set for merged PRs too.
State-transition vocab event (#524).

created_at.description:
PR creation time (ISO-8601, `gh`'s `createdAt`) — the "new PR" vocab
event of the remote-Δ freshness design (#524). `Option` +
`serde(default)` with the `AimWire::working_delta` discipline so
older payloads (hub fixtures, pre-sync clients) stay deserializable.

merge_commit_sha.description:
Kept for shape parity with `PrInfo`. `null` in practice even for the
merged rows of the recent-transition window — the unit-list fetch
doesn't request `mergeCommit`; the lifecycle fact is `state` +
`merged_at`.

merged_at.description:
Merge time (ISO-8601) — `null` unless merged. State-transition vocab
event (#524).

producer_reviewed.description:
`true` when the Producer has **delivered** its contract-anchored
Δ-brief for this PR — the soft-valve unlock condition of dev-loop
DR `2026-05-16-dev-loop-completes-in-tmai` §E (approach
`2026-05-17-producer-review-gated-in-tmai-merge`).

**Delivered, NOT approved.** A neutral delivered-marker: it states
only that the brief reached the operator, never that the Producer
approves/blocks the merge or judges it merge-worthy (§E load-bearing
boundary — the Producer's opinion is *content of the brief*, never
this bit; the merge decision stays wholly the operator's). The react
half consumes this to make the reviewed merge path frictionless
(dialog / colour / dismissible override) — that button state machine
is a separate dispatch; core only exposes the bit, it never blocks a
merge on it. Not a `PrInfo` field: it is overlaid from the transient
[`AppState::producer_reviewed_prs`](crate::state::AppState) in
[`build_unit_prs_response`], so it resets on engine restart (the
Producer re-briefs).

`default` + `skip_serializing_if` keep it optional with the exact
#404 `is_producer` discipline: the field is absent on the wire when
`false`, and a payload omitting it deserializes to `false` — a
client omitting it stays lockstep-free.

review_decision.description:
Raw `gh` review-decision string (`"APPROVED"` / `"CHANGES_REQUESTED"`
/ `"REVIEW_REQUIRED"`), or `null` when GitHub reports none.

ProjectedStatusWire.description:
A record's fact-projected status, mirroring [`ProjectedStatus`].

kebab-case on the wire (`not-started` / `running` / `accepted` /
`abandoned`) — byte-identical to [`ProjectedStatus::label`].

QueueAgentEntry.description: Per-agent view of the prompt queue.

agent_display_label.description: Best display label for the agent at snapshot time

agent_stable_id.description: tmux pane target (e.g. "main:0.1") — the key used in `AppState.prompt_queue`

oldest_queued_at.description: When the oldest prompt was enqueued

queue.description: Ordered list of queued prompts (oldest first)

total_count.description: Total number of queued prompts (== queue.len())

QueueSnapshot.description:
Snapshot of the global prompt queue, one entry per agent that has
at least one queued prompt.

entries.description: One entry per agent that currently has queued prompts (oldest-first within each agent)

QueuedPrompt.description:
Owned snapshot of a `MonitoredAgent`, returned by query methods.

All string fields are cloned out of the locked state so that callers
A prompt entry held in an agent's pending queue

id.description: UUID v7 assigned at enqueue time (time-ordered, stable cancel key)

oneOf.description: Origin of the enqueue request

prompt.description: The prompt text to be delivered when the agent becomes idle

RepoAimsWire.description:
One repo's projected aim-tree slice. Mirrors
[`super::approaches_view::RepoApproachesWire`]'s wrapper shape — the
React section passes `repo_root` as the marker `repoPath` (same as
decisions / approaches, #493). The list carries **every**
aim record in `<repo>/doc/aims/`, sorted ascending by slug.

aims.description:
Every aim record in this repo, sorted ascending by slug. The tree shape
lives in the per-node `parent:` links; the client reconstructs it.

RepoApproachesWire.description:
One repo's projected approaches slice. The list carries **every**
approach record in `<repo>/doc/approaches/` regardless of status —
`Planned` / `Partial` / `Ready` / `Running` / `Validated` / `Rejected`
/ `Replaced` — sorted most-recent-first by slug. Each [`ApproachWire`]
carries its `status` field as the discriminator the operator dashboard
filters / sorts on client-side, per #462 Amendment 2026-05-28's
dashboard 全件可視化 direction. (The Producer-side markdown's
`▣ Running approaches` bucket narrows to `Running` only — a separate
surface served by `compose::RepoSection::running_approaches`.)

approaches.description:
Every approach record in this repo, sorted most-recent-first by
slug — full lifecycle, no filter. The dashboard filters by `status`
client-side.

RepoDecisionsWire.description:
One repo's projected `Settled` section. The bucketed lists are
already temperature-sorted; the renderer (React) iterates in order.

repo_head.description: Short HEAD sha of `repo_root`, or `None` when it is not a git repo.

repo_label.description:
Path leaf of the repo root, e.g. `"tmai-core"`. Drives the group
header in the React renderer.

repo_root.description:
Absolute repo root as a string (filesystem path). Surfaced for
disambiguation and click-through; not parsed by the client.

RepoFilesWire.description: One repo's file listing within a unit (one entry per `unit.repos[]`).

path.description:
Absolute repo root path (the same `repos[]` path the decisions / PR /
units endpoints key on).

primary.description: `true` for the unit's primary repo (where the Producer is launched).

tree.description: The repo's file tree (empty when the repo path is not a directory).

RepoIssuesWire.description:
One repo's slice of the unit-scoped issue list. `repo_label` is the stable
directory basename (e.g. `"tmai-core"`) — the same scheme
[`crate::config::ResolvedUnit::from_dir`] uses for unit names; `repo_path`
is the absolute path of the repo the issues belong to.

Unlike [`crate::api::RepoPrsWire`] there is no `billing_dead` flag —
issues have no merge/CI gate to override.

issues.description:
Issues in this repo, newest first (descending issue number): the open
ones plus those closed within the recent-transition window (#524) —
`state` + `closed_at` distinguish them.

primary.description: `true` for the unit's primary repo (where the Producer is launched).

RepoPrsWire.description:
One repo's slice of the unit-scoped PR list. `repo_label` is the stable
directory basename (e.g. `"tmai-core"`) — the same scheme
[`crate::config::ResolvedUnit::from_dir`] uses for unit names; `repo_path`
is the absolute path the diff/merge endpoints take as their `repo` param.

billing_dead.description:
`true` when `[github.<repo>] billing_dead = true` is set for this
repo (`Settings::repo_billing_dead`). Surfaced so the react UI can
decide *where* to show the billing-dead CI-gate override affordance
(approach `2026-05-20-billing-dead-ci-safe-override`, O1-b) — the
affordance belongs only on a repo the operator has flagged. This is
a read-only projection of operator config; the backend
(`validate_billing_dead_override`) is the enforcing gate, never this
bit. Overlaid in [`build_unit_prs_response`] from an injected
predicate, so the function stays pure / IO-free.

`default` + `skip_serializing_if` keep it optional with the exact
#404 `is_producer` discipline: absent on the wire when `false`, and a
payload omitting it deserializes to `false` — clients stay lockstep-free.

primary.description: `true` for the unit's primary repo (where the Producer is launched).

prs.description:
PRs in this repo, newest first (descending PR number): the open ones
plus those that left the open state within the recent-transition
window (#524) — `state` + the timestamp fields distinguish them.

RepoSlackWire.description:
One repo's slack-ore slice. Repo tag fields follow
[`super::pr_view::RepoPrsWire`] (`repo_path` / `repo_label` / `primary`).

ores.description:
Every ore in this repo's `doc/slack/`, ascending by ticket (= capture
order). Empty = the repo hasn't adopted slack (or holds none) — the
normal state, not an error. The wire stays neutral: list length is
implicit, and no unread/new marker exists by design.

primary.description: `true` for the unit's primary repo (where the Producer is launched).

RetryHint.description:
Advisory retry guidance attached to a [`TmaiError`].

The hint is informational only — the caller decides whether to act on it.
Retry orchestration is explicitly out of scope for v1 of the taxonomy.

oneOf.description:
Retry after a specific wall-clock instant (RFC3339).

Used when the underlying resource knows when availability resumes
(e.g. vendor rate-limit reset).

resume_at.description: ISO-8601 / RFC3339 timestamp at which the caller may retry.

oneOf.description: Retry after a millisecond backoff (caller-driven clock).
The failure is not retryable; the caller should surface it instead.

ReviewExtensionWire.description: One entry in an approach's extend ledger, wire-projected.

extended_on.description: ISO-8601 date the extension was recorded.

ReviewTriggerWire.description:
One review trigger, wire-projected. Tagged on `kind` so the TS shape
matches the Rust [`crate::workbench::ReviewTrigger`] one-for-one;
`Date.value` becomes an ISO-8601 string on the wire (the underlying
`NaiveDate` is private to the parser).

oneOf.description: Calendar fallback — `value` is `YYYY-MM-DD`.
`reference` is `<owner>/<repo>#<number>`.
`reference` is `<owner>/<repo>#<number>`.
`reference` is `<owner>/<repo>#<number>`.

`reference` is a decision-record slug; `target_status` matches the
`DecisionStatus` wire enum (`accepted` / `proposed` / etc.).

`reference` is an approach-record slug; `target_status` matches the
`ApproachStatus` wire enum (`validated` / `rejected` / etc.).

Never auto-fires. `description` records the operator's intent for a
future Producer suggestion pass.

RuntimeSnapshot.description: UI-facing snapshot of runtime infrastructure state.

vendors.description: Availability state of each tracked AI vendor.

ServingHealthWire.description:
The per-decision running-means verdict over its serving approaches,
mirroring [`ServingHealth`].

kebab-case on the wire (`orphaned` / `no-running-means` / `healthy` /
`overflow`). A mechanical classification of `(serving, running)` counts —
NOT a severity (the UI decides how to render each verdict).

SlackCaptureRequest.description:
`POST /api/units/{unit}/slack` request — capture one ore.

**Capture is text only** (operator-ratified): no care-level, no category,
no appraisal field of any kind. The server derives the ticket from the
capture time and writes `text` verbatim to
`<repo_path>/doc/slack/<ticket>.md`. The writer is the operator via UI for
now — no MCP tool in this stage (§6d: agent 側残余は諦め inventory 管轄).

repo_path.description:
Target repo — must be one of the unit's `repos[]` paths (the same
spelling `RepoSlackWire::repo_path` serves; anything else is `422`,
never a write outside the unit).

text.description: The ore's whole asserted content. Empty / whitespace-only is `422`.

SlackOreWire.description:
One slack ore projected to the wire — the read complement of the internal
[`crate::workbench::SlackRecord`].

`quoted_by` is the **derived** quoted/unmined edge (never a stored field):
the same-repo aim slugs whose record text cites this ore's file
([`crate::workbench::quoted_by`]). Empty = unmined. Both states are normal
— an ore that never becomes an aim is正規 (§6c).

body.description:
The raw text body — the entire ore file, verbatim. Capture is text
only: this is the ore's whole asserted content.

captured_at.description:
Capture time derived from the ticket (ISO-8601 seconds, no zone —
the ticket carries local capture time). Not a stored field.

quoted_by.description:
Slugs of the same-repo aims that quote this ore (derived on read,
never stored). Empty = unmined. `default` so a payload omitting it
still deserializes.

ticket.description:
Filename stem — the capture-timestamp parking ticket
(`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS`, optional `-N` collision suffix). A parking
ticket, not an identity: nothing references an ore by name until an
aim quotes it.

SpawnerHandle.description:
Who or what spawned a dispatch. For orchestrator hierarchies (#9) this
chain is what lets a downstream observer attribute work back to its
originating human or parent dispatch.

oneOf.description: Spawned by another dispatch (e.g. an orchestrator delegating).
Spawned directly by a human via a UI surface.

StaleSinceWire.description:
The drift evidence for one decision: which `governs:` path moved and
the commit that moved it.

TerminalSubscription.description:
Owned response for `POST /api/agents/{id}/subscribe-terminal`.

Mirrors `tmai_pty_server::proto::TerminalTicket` augmented with an
optional `stream_endpoint` describing where the UI should connect.

agent_id.description: Canonical agent id (`<scheme>:<id>`) the ticket is bound to.

expires_at.description: Wall-clock expiry time (RFC3339). UIs re-request before this.

stream_endpoint.description:
Relative URL of the WebSocket-to-Unix bridge for browser UIs
(Phase 2b). UIs `WebSocket(<server>/<stream_endpoint>?ticket=<token>&mode=stream|keys)`
to receive ANSI bytes (or send key bytes) over a binary frame
stream. `None` only when running against a Phase 2a-era server
that has not yet wired the bridge — UIs MUST tolerate `None` and
either fall back to the legacy `/api/agents/{id}/terminal` WS or
connect to the local `terminal.sock` directly.

token.description:
Opaque bearer token. UI presents this as the first frame on the
terminal-plane socket. Treat as a credential.

TmaiError.description:
Structured error carried across every tmai contract boundary.

The shape is stable: `code` is the primary dispatch key; `message` is
human-readable; `retry_hint` is advisory; `context` is per-code
structured detail; `trace_id` ties the error to a `tracing` span for
cross-surface correlation.

context.description:
Code-specific structured detail. Always an object in practice; defaults
to `null` when the caller did not attach any.

Wire: omitted when null (see `skip_serializing_if`). TS: rendered as
`unknown`, which already accepts `undefined` at the call site — no
`ts(optional)` because that attribute rejects non-`Option<T>` fields.

message.description: Human-readable summary. English-only in v1 (shape supports future i18n).

oneOf.description: Advisory retry guidance; absent when the caller has no way to retry.

trace_id.description:
Request/span identifier for correlating this error across MCP, WebUI,
and internal tracing spans.

TriageVerdict.description:
How a synthesis-pass triage routed a single input (worker friction note,
drift-audit verdict, etc.). Matches DR §B.1's three-way routing.

UnitFilesResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/files` response — one listing per unit repo,
primary-first then declaration order (the `unit_repo_order` convention the
PR / units views share).

UnitInventoryResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/inventory` response — the unit's nested in-play
inventory, mirroring [`Inventory`].

Every decision (sorted by slug, serving approaches nested) followed by the
unanchored approaches. `today` is the ISO reference date liveness was
computed against (so the R-panel can render an "as of" without guessing the
server clock).

today.description: ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` reference date the liveness was computed against.

UnitIssuesResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/issues` response — the unit's open issues across
every repo in `repos[]`, grouped per repo (primary first, then declaration
order). A unified cross-repo surface, mirroring [`crate::api::UnitPrsResponse`]:
the React side renders one flat list, not a per-repo switcher.

For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1`.

UnitPrsResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/prs` response — the unit's open (+ recently
transitioned, #524) PRs across every repo in `repos[]`, grouped per repo
(primary first, then declaration order). A unified cross-repo surface per
DR §A: the React side renders one flat list, not a per-repo switcher.

For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1`.

UnitRepoWire.description:
One repo of a unit's resolved membership: its absolute path plus whether
it is the unit's **primary** repo (the one at
[`ResolvedUnit::repo_root`] / `primary_index`, where the Producer is
launched).

`primary` is a per-repo bool, deliberately **not** a separate
`primary_index` field on the parent: the client joins these repos with
live agents by `path`, so it needs only the per-row "is this the Producer's
repo?" bit, never an index into a list it never indexes. Keeps the wire
minimal (issue #439 contract boundary).

path.description:
Absolute repo root path (the same `repos[]` path the decisions / PR
endpoints key on, so the client can join membership across surfaces).

primary.description: `true` for the unit's primary repo (where the Producer is launched).

UnitResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}` response — one unit's repo membership.

`repos` is ordered **primary first, then declaration order** — the same
convention [`unit_repo_order`] gives the cross-repo PR view, so the two
unit surfaces stay consistent. For a single-repo unit this collapses to
`repos.len() == 1`.

UnitSlackResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/slack` response — the unit's slack ores across
every repo in `repos[]`, grouped per repo (primary first). Read on demand,
no cache. For a single-repo unit this collapses to `repos.len() == 1`.

UnitsResponse.description:
`GET /api/units` response — every configured unit's membership.

Empty `units` when no `[[unit]]` is configured (the workbench then
operates on a single cwd-synthesized unit, which is not enumerated here —
this surface reflects *configured* membership only).

Vendor.description: Vendor CLI identifier for dispatched agents.

VendorAvailabilityState.description:
Vendor availability rollup reported by
[`CoreEvent::VendorAvailabilityChanged`].

`Available` → normal operation.
`RateLimited` → vendor returned 429 / Max-plan limit; `resume_at` is the
advertised reset time when known.
`Unavailable` → outage or auth failure; retry left to policy.

oneOf.description: Vendor is accepting dispatches.
Rate-limited; retry after `resume_at` if present.

resume_at.description: Wall-clock time when dispatches may resume (if known).

oneOf.description: Vendor is fully unavailable (outage, auth failure).

reason.description: Free-text reason for the outage (no secrets).

VendorInfo.description: Availability information for a single AI vendor.

account.description: Account label when the deployment distinguishes multiple accounts.

last_changed_at.description: When the availability state last changed.

WorkResidualWire.description:
The outstanding work-residual: the unconfirmed/unabandoned id set the #484
`work_residual` projection yields, plus its `count` so the React side need
not recompute `outstanding.len()`. `outstanding` is sorted + de-duplicated
by the engine.

WorkflowSnapshot.description:
UI-facing snapshot of workflow-level orchestration state.

The per-event `notify_mode_summary` / `orchestrator_notify_backlog` were
removed with the legacy observe-based auto-wake host (2026-05-26 amendment
to the producer-worker-identity approach, §rip): the
`orchestrator_notify`-derived echo taxonomy and busy-notify buffer that
fed them are gone. What survives is the orchestrator-identity projection.

orchestrator_agent_id.description: Stable agent id of the active orchestrator, if one is running.

WorkingWithHumanResponse.description:
`GET /api/units/{unit}/working-with-human` response.

`dir = None` → the unit has no resolvable memory directory (no
override, no auto-derived `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory`). The
section reads "no memory dir configured for this unit" rather than
fabricating a synthetic baseline. `dir = Some` + `memory_index =
None` → the directory exists but contains no `MEMORY.md`; the
section reads "(no `MEMORY.md` found under <dir>)".

dir.description:
Absolute path of the resolved memory directory (display-only).
`None` when no memory dir exists for this unit.

memory_index.description:
Raw `MEMORY.md` contents — markdown index pointing at per-entry
memory files. `None` when no `MEMORY.md` is present in `dir`.

WorktreeInfo.description:
Worktree information attached to hook events in `--worktree` sessions

Contains name, path, branch, and the original repo directory.
Added in Claude Code v2.1.69.

branch.description: Branch checked out in the worktree

WorktreeSnapshot.description: Owned snapshot of a worktree for API consumers

agent_pending.description: Whether an agent is pending detection (recently spawned, not yet linked)

oneOf.description: Diff statistics vs base branch

is_dirty.description: Whether this worktree has uncommitted changes

is_main.description: Whether this is the main working tree

oneOf.description: Active non-terminal dispatch working in this worktree

repo_path.description: Absolute path to the git common directory

description:
Push-based state-change events emitted by tmai-core and streamed to UI clients over SSE at `/api/events` (and fanned out to MCP and audit consumers). Every variant is discriminated by the `type` field (internally tagged) and carries event-specific fields at the top level.

## Forward-compatibility rules

UI clients MUST ignore unknown `type` values — new variants will be added as non-breaking changes. New optional fields SHOULD be tolerated silently. Required field changes, field-type changes, or variant removals trigger a major version bump of this spec.

## Taxonomy cross-reference

Contract-layer events (`DispatchRejected`, `ContractViolation`) embed a `TmaiError` / `ErrorCode` whose shape is defined in `openapi.json` → `components.schemas.TmaiError`. The shapes are kept in sync — see `docs/errors.md` for authoritative documentation.

oneOf.description: Claude Code configuration file was changed

CLAUDE.md or `.claude/rules/*.md` files were loaded into context

Added in Claude Code v2.1.69. Fires when instruction files are loaded.
An agent stopped (completed or paused), emitted by hook handler

An agent's `AgentId` was promoted from `Provisional` to a native
session id once the underlying CLI reported one (Phase 1 of #96).

Subscribers that hold references to the previous id should rewrite
them to `to`. The wire form of `from` and `to` is `<scheme>:<id>`
(e.g. `"claude:ea760770-..."`). `target` is the tmux pane id and
remains the legacy lookup key during Phase 1.

from.description: Previous id, always a `provisional:<uuid>` in Phase 1

oneOf.description: Context compaction started (PreCompact hook event)

compaction_count.description: How many compactions have occurred in this session

oneOf.description: Worktree setup commands completed successfully
Worktree setup commands failed
A queued prompt is ready to be delivered (agent transitioned to Idle)
A tool call was deferred for external resolution
A worktree branch was successfully rebased onto the default branch
A worktree branch rebase failed due to conflicts
A deferred tool call was resolved (approved/denied)
A new PR was detected by the PR monitor
CI checks passed for a PR
CI checks failed for a PR
A PR received review feedback (changes requested)

branch.description:
Head branch name. `#[serde(default)]` keeps the field backward-
compatible on the wire, matching `PrCreated`/`PrClosed` which
already carry `branch`.

review_count.description:
Monotonic review count from PR state — distinguishes
back-to-back ChangesRequested transitions so the notifier
can dedupe on `(pr_number, review_count)` instead of
collapsing distinct rounds into one entry.

oneOf.description: A PR was closed (merged or closed without merging)

A PR has been open without any CI run starting for longer than
`OrchestrationSettings::pr_monitor_ci_start_timeout_secs`. Typically
indicates GitHub Actions silently skipped (merge conflicts blocking
CI, Actions disabled at the repo, etc.) — see #334. Counted from the
first time the monitor observed the PR; warm-up baseline PRs are
exempt so a tmai restart does not retro-fire the alarm. Emitted at
most once per PR.

waited_secs.description:
How many seconds the monitor has been watching this PR with no
CI status. Always >= the configured timeout.

oneOf.description:
Git state changed for a repository (branches, HEAD, commit graph).

Emitted by [`crate::git::monitor::GitMonitor`] when its poll detects
a transition. WebUI subscribers refetch `/api/git/*` in response so
the UI and the monitor snapshot observe the same tick (#423 — sibling
of the PR Monitor SoT pattern).
An agent's tmux target changed due to pane renumbering (PID-based reconciliation)

pid.description: Process ID that links the two targets

oneOf.description: A side-effect API action was performed (for orchestrator notification)

A dispatch request was rejected by the contract-layer gatekeeper
before an agent was spawned. Subscribers never saw this intent as an
`AgentAppeared` / `AgentStatusChanged` — they learn about it only via
this event. Payload is redacted: see [`DispatchIntentSummary`].

intent_summary.description: Redacted description of what was dispatched.

oneOf.description:
Vendor availability transitioned (rate-limit hit or cleared, outage
started or resolved). Subscribers can refresh capacity badges or
resume queued dispatches without polling the vendor directly.

account.description: Account label, if the deployment distinguishes accounts.

detected_via.description: How the transition was detected (hook, polling, explicit signal).

oneOf.description:
The global capacity counter moved (a slot was consumed or freed)
or the configured limit was adjusted. `delta` is the change applied
to `current`; `cause` attributes the change without requiring a
follow-up query.

current.description: New value of the in-use slot counter.

delta.description:
Signed change applied to `current` by this event
(+1 on spawn, -1 on terminal, 0 on `LimitChanged`/`Reconciled`).

limit.description: Configured ceiling at the time of emission.

oneOf.description:
A contract-layer invariant was violated (auth failure, schema
mismatch, unauthorized operation, etc.). Distinct from
`DispatchRejected` which is gatekeeper-specific (#463, open q. 4).

context.description:
Code-specific structured context (never includes secrets or
raw prompts). Defaults to `null` when the emitter has nothing
to attach.

oneOf.description:
A bypass flag was used for a dispatch (skipped one or more
contract-layer checks). Always audit-logged; subscribers can
use this to surface a banner or alert.

bypassed.description:
Names of the bypassed checks / stages (e.g.
"capacity_check", "vendor_availability").

reason.description: Free-text justification supplied by the caller.

oneOf.description: A single agent was upserted (appeared / changed state) or removed.

change.description: Whether the agent was upserted or removed.

id.description:
Canonical AgentId (`<scheme>:<id>` form, matches `AgentSnapshot.id`).
Stable across tmux pane renumber, `claude --resume`, tmai restart, and
process restart. Provisional ids (`provisional:<uuid>`) are normally
hidden but become wire-visible when the agent has a `pty_session_id`
(PTY-server backed spawn).

oneOf.description: Who or what triggered this update.

A dispatch ledger entry was upserted or removed.

Emitted alongside `DispatchSpawned` / `DispatchStateChanged` /
`DispatchStopped` during Phase 1.

change.description: Whether the dispatch was upserted or removed.

oneOf.description: Who or what triggered this update.

A worktree snapshot was upserted or removed.

Emitted alongside `WorktreeCreated` / `WorktreeRemoved` during Phase 1.

change.description: Whether the worktree was upserted or removed.

oneOf.description: Who or what triggered this update.

The global prompt queue changed (enqueue / drain / cancel).

Emitted alongside `PromptQueueChanged` during Phase 1.
Carries the full [`super::types::QueueSnapshot`] so consumers can
replace their local snapshot directly.
Who or what triggered this update.

Runtime infrastructure state changed.

Emitted when `CapacityChanged`, `VendorAvailabilityChanged`,
or `ContextCompacting` mutate the runtime view.
Who or what triggered this update.

allOf.description:
A Producer handoff-and-restart ritual transitioned phases (DR
`2026-05-14-handoff-lifecycle-and-kill-ux.md` §C/§F). Emitted by the
`POST /api/units/{unit}/handoff-and-restart` endpoint on every step;
streamed to SSE consumers as event kind `handoff_ritual`.

oneOf.description:
A Producer handoff-and-restart ritual transitioned phases (DR
`2026-05-14-handoff-lifecycle-and-kill-ux.md` §C/§F). Emitted by the
`POST /api/units/{unit}/handoff-and-restart` endpoint on every step;
streamed to SSE consumers as event kind `handoff_ritual`.

title: CoreEvent
